reason: Backend MCP/server implementation work for future follow-up tasks should start from the canonical layer checklist and linked quality rules.
This assessment identifies connection, observability, and failure-contract gaps; future implementation must preserve MCP-safe error behavior.
A primary gap is missing real diagnostics and log capture, so future work needs the backend logging discipline.
Follow-up tasks will likely restructure tests, tool responses, and validation, which should stay aligned with backend quality expectations.
The identified problems span MCP server, WebSocket transport, plugin UI, and Godot runtime feedback, so cross-layer reasoning is required.
